feature_name: Audio thumbnail-as-background art (feasibility-gated) + fuzzy-similar related items

acceptance.status: ACCEPTED (automated) — ready for PR (v1.10.0). Both features' automated AC covered by the 241-test suite (green) + lint 0. The two [MANUAL] on-device items are DEFERRED to Dean's pass against the PR — documented pending, NON-BLOCKING.
acceptance.feature2_related: rankRelated (title/filename/tags token overlap + shared-folder + cross-folder same-channel, weights/N as named constants). Automated [UNIT] AC covered by the suite (similar-above-unrelated, self-excluded, deterministic+stable total-order tie-break, never-empty/never-worse fallback below SIMILAR_FLOOR=6, tokenization drops stopwords/short tokens, edge cases). QA-APPROVED with the folded W_CHANNEL/'Library'-bare-default fix (guard on real channel signal both sides) + degenerate-case test. The [MANUAL] 'feels related on real content' AC = Dean's on-device call; weights retunable without code churn.
acceptance.feature1_audio_art: Audio thumbnail-as-background art (CSS #audio-bg-art layer behind a transparent player, AUDIO_PLAYER_MODE one-line fallback to the retained #audio-visualizer). Automated [UNIT] resolveAudioArtUrl AC covered (7 tests: real-thumbnail URL vs null placeholder/missing-id/null-item). Non-regression verified in build: video-frame/object-fit/letterbox/playsinline/v1.2.2-background-audio/lock-screen Media Session all untouched. The [MANUAL] works-case-vs-fallback is DEAN'S ON-DEVICE iOS PASS as the arbiter: art shows during playback = win; iOS paints black = flip AUDIO_PLAYER_MODE to 'visualizer' (one-line change). Feasibility finding recorded in the exec plan Design section (poster insufficient by construction -> CSS-bg -> iOS win-or-fallback; desktop/PWA wins regardless).
acceptance.manual_pending: TWO [MANUAL] items DEFERRED to Dean's on-device pass against the PR (both NON-BLOCKING): (1) F2 'related list feels related on real content'; (2) F1 iOS in-page audio background art shows during playback (win) vs. flip to the #audio-visualizer fallback (arbiter decision). Both are the documented [MANUAL] designation; automated + build-verified prerequisites are all green.
acceptance.nice_to_have: AUDIO_PLAYER_MODE iOS-only-UA-gate (PE's optional refinement) — if Dean wants desktop art AND iOS fallback SIMULTANEOUSLY, gate the fallback to iOS via a UA check so desktop keeps the CSS art. Acceptance-time refinement / nice-to-have, tracked in the exec plan Decision log; not built (the default flips globally, one line).
acceptance.recorded_by: EM, per coordinator instruction to close out; final user acceptance = Dean's on-device pass on the PR.

description: ONE branch, TWO independent media-experience features kept as SEPARATE commits/tasks so they revert independently. FEATURE 1 (FEASIBILITY-GATED, UI/feasibility-heavy, mostly [MANUAL]): make an AUDIO-only file's item THUMBNAIL show as the in-page player background/art on iOS Safari during PLAYBACK, so audio 'looks like a video is playing' (video files already show their frame fine). Discovery/Design MUST include a FEASIBILITY SPIKE: can mobile Safari render the thumbnail behind/around an audio-only <video> DURING playback? Candidate approaches to investigate: (a) <video> poster attr for audio items (NOTE: watch.js:288-295 already sets mediaPlayer.poster = /thumbnail/:id for audio and hides #audio-visualizer — poster typically vanishes once playback starts, which is exactly the reported iOS black screen); (b) a CSS background-image (thumbnail) on the player-container behind a transparent/again-poster'd player; (c) whether iOS's native audio takeover forces black regardless. The lock-screen Media Session artwork (setupMediaSession, watch.js:249-265) is the LOCK SCREEN, not the in-page player — do NOT confuse them. If achievable on iOS: implement thumbnail-as-audio-background (object-fit:cover so it reads like video), without regressing how VIDEO files display. If NOT achievable (iOS forces black): degrade gracefully to the existing PWA custom #audio-visualizer (vinyl/cover-art view, watch.html:139-145, currently display:none) as the documented PWA-only fallback — user ACCEPTS this. AC must cover BOTH the works-case AND the graceful-fallback case and STATE the feasibility finding explicitly. Preserve the v1.2.2 iOS background-audio behavior and playsinline inline-playback behavior — do NOT regress. FEATURE 2 (additive, real [UNIT] + QA gate): make 'Related Files' feel actually RELATED, not just most-recent. Currently loadRelatedFiles (watch.js:710-753) fetches /api/videos (newest-first), filters out current, prioritizes same folderName, slices 10. Design a LIGHTWEIGHT similarity ranking: a PURE, exported, UNIT-TESTABLE rankRelated(currentItem, allItems) -> ordered list scoring other items by fuzzy/keyword similarity (candidate signals for the PE to choose: title/filename token overlap, shared folder, shared artist/channel/tags), rank by score, FALL BACK to most-recent when fewer than N similar items so the list is NEVER empty/worse than today. 'Nothing crazy' — a basic tokenize + score, NOT a search engine. Home for the pure fn: frontend helpers already use a browser+node UMD export (common.js:483) and are node:tested (resolve-theme, clamp-position-state, star-rating) — rankRelated can be a shared client-side pure fn fed by the existing /api/videos with NO new server endpoint (PE decides exact home). Wire into the existing related-items population; keep the current rendering + non-empty guarantee. Repo: Node 22, node:test (npm test), ESLint 0 errors (11 'defined but never used' exported-globals warnings = allowed baseline), additive/zero-regression, ships to deantammam/filetube on a v*.*.* tag. Feature 2's ranking warrants real unit tests + the quality-assurance agent (+ a code-review pass if the PE finds the scoring subtle). Feature 1 is UI/feasibility-heavy and mostly [MANUAL] — build-verify + Dean's on-device pass.
npm_env_note: Every node/npm command in this repo needs `export PATH="/home/coder/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v24.14.0/installation/bin:$PATH"` first (fnm not auto-sourced). Applies to specialist agents running npm test / npm run lint.
prior_release: v1.9.0 (harden/db-writes-and-logo: structural db.json write hardening + mobile logo top-left) shipped; state archived by overwrite, exec plan in docs/exec-plans/completed/2026-07-05-harden-db-writes-and-logo.md. The 217-test suite is the green baseline.
out_of_scope: The 'optional yt-dlp integration module' (docs/exec-plans/future/yt-dlp-integration-module.md, pointed to by ROADMAP) is the branch-AFTER-next, explicitly PARKED — do NOT act on it in this branch.

tasks.title: Feature 2 (commit 1, FIRST): rankRelated similarity ranking + tokenize in common.js + loadRelatedFiles wiring + unit tests
tasks.description: Additive, pure-logic + QA-gated. Add to public/js/common.js (inside the UMD dual-export block ~483, node:test-able like resolveTheme/getStarRating): (1) a PRIVATE tokenize(str) — lowercase; split /[^a-z0-9]+/; drop tokens <2 chars; drop the fixed small stopword set (the a an and or of to in on for with feat ft official video audio hd mp3 mp4 avi mkv mov webm); dedupe (return a Set); missing/empty input contributes nothing, never throws. An item's token set = union of tokenize(title), tokenize(basename of filePath), and each tags entry tokenized (tags optional/absent/non-array = nothing). (2) rankRelated(currentItem, allItems) -> ordered array, best-match first, self EXCLUDED by id (item.id !== currentItem.id). Named constants: RESULT_COUNT=10, SIMILAR_FLOOR=6, W_TOKEN=3, W_FOLDER=2, W_CHANNEL=1. Score per candidate = W_TOKEN*|sharedTokens| + W_FOLDER (folderName non-empty && equal) + W_CHANNEL (resolveChannelName(current)===resolveChannelName(candidate) with NO folderSettings AND folders differ). Explicit TOTAL-ORDER comparator (never relies on sort stability): score DESC -> addedAt DESC (missing addedAt = oldest) -> id ASC. Fallback/padding: similar = candidates score>0 sorted by comparator; recent = ALL candidates sorted addedAt DESC, id ASC; result = de-duplicated [...similar, ...recent] sliced to RESULT_COUNT — never empty, never worse than today; SIMILAR_FLOOR=6 is the documented guarantee point (when <6 clear score>0 the shortfall fills from the recent tail = today's behavior). Export rankRelated (and tokenize if convenient for testing) from the UMD block. WIRING: in watch.js loadRelatedFiles (~710-753), REPLACE the block that filters current id + splits sameFolder/otherFolders + slice(0,10) (~715-722) with `const related = rankRelated({ ...mediaData, id: mediaId }, allFiles);`. Leave the `related.length === 0` empty-state guard and the ENTIRE .related-card render loop (thumbnail/title/folder/duration badge/mock views) UNCHANGED. Ensure common.js is already loaded before watch.js on watch.html (it is: common.js then watch.js at watch.html:259-260). Add test/unit/rank-related.test.js. Scoped OUT: Feature 1 (=T2); no new endpoint/DB field/dependency; no change to the render loop or empty guard.
tasks.done_when: rankRelated + tokenize implemented in common.js per the design contract (named constants, explicit total-order comparator, self-excluded by id, fallback padding to never-empty). loadRelatedFiles rewired to the single rankRelated call with the empty guard + .related-card render loop byte-unchanged. test/unit/rank-related.test.js covers the FULL [UNIT] set: (1) similar-above-unrelated (shared title/filename tokens and/or folder rank above token-disjoint); (2) self excluded (current id in allItems never appears); (3) deterministic + stable tie-break (equal scores order addedAt DESC then id ASC; repeated calls byte-identical arrays); (4) fallback below floor (<6 similar + many unrelated -> full min(10, others) list, similar first then most-recent, never empty); (5) tokenization drops stopwords + <2-char tokens (items sharing only 'the'/'a' etc. NOT similar); (6) edge cases: empty allItems -> []; library of one (only self) -> []; items missing tags/artist/folderName/filePath do not throw and preserve self-exclusion + never-empty. Additive/zero-regression: render loop + empty guard unchanged. Full suite green (217 + new); lint 0 (no new warnings beyond the 11 baseline); PATH export before npm. Gets the quality-assurance agent (+ a code-review pass if scoring looks subtle).
tasks.result: DONE + build-verified + QA-APPROVED (coordinator-relayed), committed d583ded. QA verdict APPROVE with one small WARNING (the W_CHANNEL/'Library'-bare-default gap: two items both missing artist+folderName spuriously matching via resolveChannelName's 'Library' fallback string) — folded into the T1 commit: SDE guarded W_CHANNEL on REAL channel signal on both sides (not the 'Library' string) + added a SIMILAR_FLOOR clarity comment + a new degenerate-case unit test. Final: lint 0; npm test 234/234. Verified: explicit total-order comparator (score DESC -> addedAt DESC -> id ASC), self-exclusion by id, never-empty/never-worse fallback (similar score>0 first, padded from most-recent tail, SIMILAR_FLOOR=6), tags-object contribution on real data (artist/album feed similarity), resolveChannelName routing, .related-card render-loop + empty guard byte-unchanged, O(n) perf. Weights/N are named constants Dean can retune. The [MANUAL] 'feels related on real content' AC remains Dean's on-device call (acceptance).

tasks.title: Feature 1 (commit 2, after T1): audio thumbnail-as-background art (#audio-bg-art + CSS + .audio-mode toggle + AUDIO_PLAYER_MODE + resolveAudioArtUrl) + unit test
tasks.description: SEPARATE commit from T1, after T1 build-verifies. Approach (b) CSS background-image, feasibility-gated (Dean on-device iOS is the arbiter; desktop/PWA wins regardless). (1) watch.html: add <div id="audio-bg-art" class="audio-bg-art"> as the FIRST child of #player-wrapper (before the overlays and <video>); no change to #media-player / playsinline / #audio-visualizer. (2) style.css: add .audio-bg-art (position:absolute; inset:0; z-index:0; background-size:cover; background-position:center; display:none) and #player-wrapper.audio-mode #media-player { background:transparent; z-index:1; }; the .player-container #000 bg + `video { object-fit:contain }` rules stay UNCHANGED so art only shows for opted-in audio. (3) common.js: add + export pure resolveAudioArtUrl(item) -> '/thumbnail/'+item.id when item && item.id && item.hasThumbnail truthy, else null (placeholder-only skips the art layer). (4) watch.js: add module-level AUDIO_PLAYER_MODE constant ('background' default shipped | 'visualizer' = retained #audio-visualizer fallback = one-line flip; optional iOS-only UA-gate is a Dean acceptance-time refinement). In setupPlayer()'s audio branch (~288-295) when AUDIO_PLAYER_MODE==='background': resolve resolveAudioArtUrl(mediaData); if a URL, set audioBgArt.style.backgroundImage + display:block + add 'audio-mode' class to #player-wrapper, keep #audio-visualizer hidden; if null, leave art hidden + fall through to today's poster behavior; keep the existing poster/src assignments. When 'visualizer': show #audio-visualizer, art hidden. Video items take NEITHER path. Add test/unit/resolve-audio-art-url.test.js (real thumbnail -> /thumbnail/abc; hasThumbnail false/missing -> null; null/undefined item -> null no throw; item without id -> null; deterministic). MUST NOT regress: video-frame display (no audio-mode/art on video), playsinline/webkit-playsinline, v1.2.2 background-audio (no Media Session action handlers added), lock-screen setupMediaSession (separate surface). Mostly [MANUAL] on-device + build-verify.
tasks.done_when: #audio-bg-art layer added as first child of #player-wrapper; .audio-bg-art + #player-wrapper.audio-mode #media-player CSS added (player-container #000/object-fit:contain untouched); resolveAudioArtUrl exported from common.js per contract; AUDIO_PLAYER_MODE constant + audio-branch toggle wired (art layer/audio-mode class for 'background', #audio-visualizer for 'visualizer'; null-URL falls through to poster); video branch untouched. test/unit/resolve-audio-art-url.test.js green. Non-regression verified: video frame display, playsinline, v1.2.2 background-audio (no action handlers), lock-screen Media Session all unchanged; desktop/PWA shows cover-framed art. Feasibility finding is recorded in the exec plan Design section (already done by PE). Full suite green (T1 count + new); lint 0; PATH export before npm. Dean's on-device iOS [MANUAL] pass is the acceptance arbiter (win-case art shows during playback / fallback-case #audio-visualizer via the one-line switch).
tasks.result: DONE + build-verified (coordinator-relayed), committed e8754f8. lint 0 (warnings 11->9, no new), npm test 241/241, clean exit. Confirmed UNTOUCHED: video-frame display / object-fit:contain / #000 letterbox / playsinline / v1.2.2 background-audio / lock-screen Media Session. #audio-bg-art art layer (z-index 0) + transparent player (z-index 1); overlays (resume:10, skip:6, speed:20) stack ABOVE the art; defensive audio->video reset prevents stale-background bleed; #audio-visualizer retained as the fallback; AUDIO_PLAYER_MODE one-line flip. resolveAudioArtUrl pure + 7 unit tests. T1 (rankRelated) untouched. Mostly [MANUAL] — Dean's on-device iOS pass is the F1 arbiter (art-shows = win; iOS-black = flip AUDIO_PLAYER_MODE to 'visualizer', one line).

grounding.feature1_audio_player: watch.js setupPlayer() audio branch (288-295): audioVisualizer.style.display='none'; mediaPlayer.style.display='block'; mediaPlayer.poster='/thumbnail/'+mediaId; mediaPlayer.src=streamUrl. So poster(a) is ALREADY the current behavior — iOS black-during-playback = poster vanishes on play. #audio-visualizer markup at watch.html:139-145 (vinyl-disc + audio-visual-title + audio-visual-folder), currently display:none. player-container id=player-wrapper (watch.html:98). setupMediaSession (watch.js:249-265) is lock-screen artwork ONLY (do not confuse). playsinline/webkit-playsinline on #media-player (watch.html:119). v1.2.2 iOS background-audio + no-custom-action-handlers rationale documented at watch.js:242-248.
grounding.thumbnail_endpoint: GET /thumbnail/:id (server.js:1605) NEVER 404s: returns the real .jpg when item.hasThumbnail && file exists, else an SVG placeholder (audio bg #2b3e50 / video #4a154b). So an audio item's /thumbnail/:id URL is always resolvable; a helper may still want to distinguish real-thumbnail vs placeholder via item.hasThumbnail.
grounding.feature2_related: loadRelatedFiles (watch.js:710-753): GET /api/videos (returns Object.values(db.metadata) mapped w/ progress, sorted addedAt DESC — newest first), filter out current id, same-folderName first then others, slice(0,10), render .related-card. Fields available per item: id,title,folderName,filePath,ext,type,size,addedAt,duration,hasThumbnail,tags,lastServedAt,transcodeStatus,progress,progressPercent.
grounding.pure_fn_pattern: Frontend pure helpers are browser+node dual via UMD at common.js:483 (`if (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports) { module.exports = {...} }`) and node:tested (test/unit/resolve-theme.test.js, clamp-position-state.test.js, star-rating.test.js, resolve-icon-set.test.js, active-nav-item.test.js, comment-count.test.js). rankRelated should follow this pattern (shared client fn, node:tested) — PE decides whether it lives in common.js, a new public/js/*.js, or server.js + a related endpoint.
grounding.test_layout: test/unit/ (pure logic, node:test) + test/integration/ (boot app on ephemeral port, isolated temp DATA_DIR). No FFmpeg in the suite. Every feature ships with tests (CONTRIBUTING). Baseline 217 tests green, lint 0 (+11 allowed exported-global warnings).

history.note: New feature initiated after v1.9.0 (harden/db-writes-and-logo) shipped/done. ONE branch feat/audio-art-and-related, TWO independent media-experience features as SEPARATE commits. Grounded both in real code before routing: (F1) watch.js:288-295 already sets mediaPlayer.poster=/thumbnail/:id for audio + hides #audio-visualizer, so approach-(a) poster is effectively already shipped and the iOS black-during-playback report is the classic 'poster vanishes on play' limitation — the feasibility spike is real and load-bearing; /thumbnail/:id (server.js:1605) never 404s (real jpg or SVG placeholder); the #audio-visualizer vinyl view (watch.html:139-145) exists but is display:none = the graceful fallback; setupMediaSession (249-265) is lock-screen-only, not the in-page player; v1.2.2 iOS background-audio + playsinline must not regress. (F2) loadRelatedFiles (watch.js:710-753) is newest-first + same-folder-first minus current = essentially most-recent; frontend pure helpers already dual-export browser+node (common.js:483) and are node:tested, so a pure rankRelated(currentItem, allItems) fed by the existing /api/videos needs NO new server endpoint (PE picks the home). Read CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, RELIABILITY.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, watch.html, watch.js, common.js, the thumbnail/api-videos routes. F1 feasibility framing + F2 similarity-signal choice).

Both features SEPARATE commits/tasks; additive/zero-regression, tests-with-every-change, lint 0, keep 217+ suite green, PATH export before npm. Awaiting PE completion, then /prep-em-tasks.

DESIGN DONE + implementation-ready (coordinator-relayed): PE wrote the ## Design section into the exec plan and set artifacts.design. Both features are PURE HELPERS in common.js — no new client module, route, DB field, or dependency; no ARCHITECTURE.md component introduced. F1 design: dedicated <div id=audio-bg-art> first child of #player-wrapper (position:absolute inset:0 z-index:0 background-size:cover center display:none); audio-mode makes #media-player background:transparent z-index:1 so cover-framed art shows behind the audio content box; only audio items get .audio-mode (video/object-fit/letterbox/playsinline/v1.2.2-bg-audio/lock-screen all untouched); AUDIO_PLAYER_MODE ('background' default | 'visualizer' fallback) = one-line flip, optional iOS-only UA-gate at acceptance; pure resolveAudioArtUrl(item) -> '/thumbnail/'+id when id && hasThumbnail, else null; feasibility finding stated (poster insufficient by construction -> CSS-bg -> iOS win-or-fallback -> Dean on-device [MANUAL] arbiter, desktop/PWA wins regardless). F2 design: rankRelated(currentItem, allItems) in common.js UMD, fed by /api/videos, replaces the sameFolder/otherFolders+slice(0,10) block in loadRelatedFiles; tokenize (lowercase, /[^a-z0-9]+/, drop <2-char + small stopword set, dedupe; item tokens = title ∪ filename-basename ∪ tags); score 3*|sharedTokens| + 2 same-non-empty-folder + 1 same-resolveChannelName-when-folders-differ; comparator score DESC -> addedAt DESC -> id ASC (explicit total order); self-excluded by id; fallback similar(score>0) first + pad from recent tail + dedup + slice RESULT_COUNT=10, SIMILAR_FLOOR=6 = never-empty guarantee; weights/N named constants (retunable at acceptance). TASK-BREAKDOWN stage (EM-owned) done: wrote the ## Task breakdown section into the exec plan and recorded T1/T2 in state.tasks — F1 and F2 as SEPARATE commits. T1 (commit 1, FIRST) = F2 rankRelated+tokenize+wiring+test/unit/rank-related.test.js (pure-logic, gets the QA gate + optional code-review); T2 (commit 2, after T1 build-verifies) = F1 audio-bg-art + CSS + .audio-mode + AUDIO_PLAYER_MODE + resolveAudioArtUrl + test/unit/resolve-audio-art-url.test.js (mostly [MANUAL] on-device + build-verify). Advanced design -> implementation; set T1 status=in-progress (T2 pending). Awaiting T1 completion, then /prep-build-verify; after T1 build-verifies -> quality-assurance gate on the F2 ranking (+ code-review if subtle) -> then T2.

Principal-engineer wrote the ## Design section into docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-05-audio-art-and-related.md and set artifacts.design to that path. Both new pure helpers live in existing common.js (UMD) — no new client module, no ARCHITECTURE.md component, no server route/DB field/dependency. F1 (approach b, feasibility-gated): stated feasibility finding — (a) poster confirmed insufficient by construction (poster only shows pre-first-frame; audio has no frame => iOS black; already shipped at watch.js:294); (b) CSS background-image is the designed, desktop/PWA-verifiable approach (dedicated <div id=audio-bg-art> first child of #player-wrapper, absolute inset:0 z-index:0 background-size:cover center, display:none default; audio <video> forced background:transparent + z-index:1 via #player-wrapper.audio-mode so the transparent audio content box shows the art cover-framed); (c) iOS-during-playback UNKNOWN in this env => delegated to Dean's on-device [MANUAL] pass as arbiter. Runtime fallback selection = a single module-level switch AUDIO_PLAYER_MODE ('background' default | 'visualizer' = retained #audio-visualizer) so an iOS-black finding is a one-line flip with zero runtime iOS detection; optional acceptance-time refinement = UA-gate the fallback to iOS-only so desktop keeps the art. Audio branch (watch.js:288-295) calls pure resolveAudioArtUrl(item) -> '/thumbnail/'+id when item.id && item.hasThumbnail else null (placeholder => skip art layer, fall back to today's poster/black; a stretched 160x90 SVG placeholder makes a poor bg). Video items never opt in (no audio-mode class/art layer) => object-fit:contain frame + #000 letterbox unchanged; playsinline/webkit-playsinline, v1.2.2 no-custom-action-handlers, and setupMediaSession lock-screen artwork all untouched. resolveAudioArtUrl in common.js + UMD export + test/unit/resolve-audio-art-url.test.js (real thumb->url, hasThumbnail false/missing->null, null item->null, no id->null, deterministic). F2: rankRelated(currentItem, allItems) in common.js (UMD), fed by existing /api/videos, replaces loadRelatedFiles filter+sameFolder/otherFolders+slice(0,10) with `const related = rankRelated({...mediaData, id: mediaId}, allFiles);` (empty guard + .related-card render loop unchanged). tokenize = lowercase, split /[^a-z0-9]+/, drop <2-char tokens + small stopword set (the a an and or of to in on for with feat ft official video audio hd mp3 mp4 avi mkv mov webm), dedupe; item token set = tokenize(title) ∪ tokenize(basename(filePath)) ∪ tags tokenized. score = W_TOKEN(3)*|sharedTokens| + W_FOLDER(2 if non-empty folderName equal) + W_CHANNEL(1 if resolveChannelName equal AND folders differ, no double-count). Comparator = score DESC, addedAt DESC, id ASC (explicit total order, no reliance on sort stability); self-excluded by id; fallback = similar(score>0) sorted, then all candidates by recency, dedup, slice RESULT_COUNT=10; SIMILAR_FLOOR=6 documented guarantee (<6 similar => padded with most-recent = today's behavior; never empty/worse). test/unit/rank-related.test.js: similar-above-unrelated, self-excluded, deterministic+stable tie-break, fallback-below-floor, tokenization (stopwords not similar), edge cases (empty list, single item = only self, missing tags/artist/folder/filePath no throw). Proposed task split (EM finalizes): T1 = F2 rankRelated helper+wiring+unit tests (commit 1, QA/code-review gate — scoring subtle); T2 = F1 CSS bg art + AUDIO_PLAYER_MODE switch + resolveAudioArtUrl + unit test (commit 2, mostly [MANUAL] + build-verify + Dean's on-device iOS pass). Order T1 then T2, separate commits, independently revertable. Two pre-existing MD024 duplicate-heading warnings (### Problem, ### Acceptance criteria across the PM's F1/F2 requirement sections) predate the Design edit and were left as-accepted at the discovery gate; the Design section's own headings are unique.
